label: Appreciation for Great Lakes Sand Dunes & Beaches

comments.text: A few geomorphology-related images from the last few years that I'm using as examples in my terrain analysis and visualization class tomorrow. 1. Dunes and beach ridges on Lake Michigan, at Pointe aux Chenes, Upper Peninsula of Michigan.

Thank you Grand Traverse Regional Land Conservancy from my 922’ depths 💙💙

Sleeping Bear Dunes is one of Michigan's most popular gems. This place is massive! The main dune climb, which is behind me, is a 284-foot-high wall of glistening white sand that tourists climb for fun. For the adventurous, you can trek onward to Lake Michigan, which is 3.5 miles of sand round trip.

The cool breeze off #lakemichigan. Waves crashing along the sandy dunes of #indianadunesnationalpark. This is the view at the end of the Dune Succession Trail 🌊 On a clear day, you can even see Chicago’s skyline way off in the distance! #nationalparks #nature #photography #travel #hiking #explore

There's no beach like an empty beach. North Beach, South Haven, Michigan. Looking west towards Lake Michigan. The "dunes" on the horizon are mounds of sand-covered ice. #photography #michigan #lakemichigan #red #bench #parkbench #southhaven #beach

I'm not sure what the explanation is for this conical sand dune in the middle of the woods. It's always fun to come up on something while hiking that makes you say "huh, wonder why that is that way?" Near Lake Superior on #NorthCountryTrail #MarchIntoLandscapes | Dune
This is a photo from the top of the dunes climb, 460 feet down to Lake Michigan.

Made this to put up in my little office. The Michigan Dune Dart (Copablepharon michiganensis) has been documented only four times ever and seems restricted to high-quality Great Lakes dunes. I probably won’t ever see this extreme rarity but maybe some day!

The Indiana Dunes National and State Parks aren't any kind of wilderness experience but they have their pleasures, although one might not be huffing up a dune trail with deep sand, even though the dune is far into dune succession. OTOH, the bird watching is great. #LakeMichigan #Indiana #birds
Sand dunes and lots of little bluestem. I love this place! #PointBeachStateForest #LakeMichigan #Wisconsin #Nature #NativePlants
Lake Michigan has beaches with sand dunes. In the middle of the continent. My coastal ass was not ready for that when I first saw it.

I don’t know why they only have one sand fence up this year; it isn’t doing nearly as well as when they have two. Half the beach is already trying to crawl into the parking lot and the playground!
#parkchat A7 already mentioned the Missouri River NRR I'm Q1 so... How about my other fave, Sleeping Bear Sand Dunes NL? 😁

Shoreline dune topography along Lake Michigan. I’ve no idea how old the dunes are. Quite rolling, ridges not always parallel to lake shore. It’s a very interesting landscape, lots of variation of habitat. The lake is behind me, obscured by more sand dunes. South of Two Rivers, WI. Near Sheboygan.

A little dune dwelling on this perfect Monday. #IndianaDunes
#LakeMichigan
Lake Michigan seen from atop the Silver Lake Sand Dunes.
The morning view from just over the dune from my campsite. #PointBeachStateForest #camping #nature #lakemichigan

The Grand Sable Dunes rise nearly 300 feet above Lake Superior, stretching for about five miles along the shoreline of Pictured Rocks National Lakeshore. Formed by ancient glacial sands and shaped by constant winds, these dunes offer sweeping views of Lake Superior and the surrounding forests.
i grew up around the great lakes so i know sand dunes but these are super different and very neat

In the shifting swells of sand dunes off the shore of Lake Michigan, author Daniel Wolff finds metaphors for social change, and in the observation of this change: hope. His "Indiana Nature Notes" are part poem, part field guide, part manifesto on the sand.
placesjournal.org/article/indi...
Lake Michigan from the top of a dune

growing up in South Bend but only having gone to [Natural] Indiana Beach (the Lake Michigan sand dunes) & not [Theme Park] Indiana Beach "beautiful Lake Shafer in Monticello, IN" remains a place of myth & legend to me. the crow mascot in their ads is very real to me though
The view from the top of the dunes to Lake Michigan is stunning. It looks like the Pacific Ocean
A1: I watch the fat bears, Old Faithful, a lighthouse on Lake Superior, and a watering hole in Namibia. #ParkChat
Beach ridges. Dune sand over beach sand. They look something like this on the ground (from Point Beach, another beach ridge complex on Lake Michigan). Second photo is the wetland between two ridges

A8 I love Sleeping Bear because I love the views of Lake Michigan. White Sands is so different than any other dunes I've experienced. The gypsum sand is more solid under your feet than regular sand, making hiking easier. #ParkChat

The Kalamazoo River, one of the largest in the State of Michigan, cut through sand dunes left from the ancient shores of a proto-lake Michigan. Along its steep banks are seeps where water flows out from underground aquifers and into the river. I don't think you can find fresher water.

Kruse Park in Muskegon, MI

Apologies for missing the ALT! First image: a large beige tent sits on the edge of a boreal forest on the sandy shore of Lake Superior. Second image: a small teardrop trailer with an arched awning sits on the tops of the sand dunes near the ocean in the Outer Banks. A small Shiba is in the shade.

Sand Dunes along Lake Superior.

Trees along the Lake Michigan dunes. Because what's not to like about trees who survive the West Winds and blowing sands?

One of the wonderful things about living near the Lake Michigan shores is the beauty of the sand dune. Sand dunes are all around us. They change day to day, depending on the strength of the wind. This is a sand dune in Ludington State Park, in Ludington, Michigan.
#pureludington #puremichigan

Under #Allegan County #Michigan lies an ancient dune system now 15 miles inland from Lake Michigan. How and when were these created? Which #glacier or glacial lake was responsible? I've been hiking all over these for years and now I WANT TO KNOW! Sol, if anyone at .bsky.social knows..

Yeah, Lake Michigan is a big difference. Also, the white sand is actually gypsum crystals and is the largest gypsum dunefield on earth. It's so fine you can actually sled down it like it's snow. Sleeping Bear is beautiful, but it's more like beach sand.

460 foot sand dunes on a lake were a bit of a surprise when I first saw them. Lake Michigan.

Silver Lake is truly a marvel. A massive dune system is trapped between Lake Michigan and Silver Lake. From the center of the dunes, you can lose sight of the water on both sides and feel like you’re in the Sahara. But from across Silver Lake, they appear to be a tiny border between lake and sky.
6 story dunes. Beauty surrounds. Bay to lake to lake. Forest becomes sand. Mighty and inspiring. #SleepingBearDunesNP #Michigan #LakeMichigan #GlenLake #naturephotgraphy #cherryrepublic
I'm in sw ON Canada, surrounded by the Great Lakes where there are many lovely sand dunes. Thanks for sharing the great photos.

The Great Lakes really adds to the scenery The Michigan side of Lake Michigan has sand dunes and it totally threw me off the first time I went and I didn’t feel like I was in Michigan for a sec even though I’ve been living here my whole life 😂

Beach front properties along the western coast of Lake Michigan have been fighting beach erosion for decades. Even dunes with grasses disappeared into the Lake. Break walls and large rip-rap have been somewhat effective. Sand, not so much.

My hometown used to have a massive dune on Lake Michigan called Pigeon Hill, also named for the carrier pigeons that gathered there. Not only are the pigeons long gone, but the hill itself was mined for its sand and leveled by the 50s. Only the name remains as a testament to our destructive hubris.
My best sleeps were in western MI, but usually right on Lake Michigan.

One feature of The Sands was its uncertain claims of property ownership. It formed as Lake Michigan gradually deposited sand against a pier built to protect a channel cut across a sandbar blocking the mouth of the Chicago River. 19/

True that! Ergo the phrase “almost legitimate.” Seeing the Lake Michigan dunes is on my bucket list – I’m on the 70 foot dunes on Cape Cod frequently but have heard so much about the Michigan ones that I want to spend some time there. Maybe 2026!
Wouldn't even need to go that far. Michigan has plenty of very large sandy dunes bordering right up to the water of lake Michigan, I imagine there's other places with similar geography

Initial setting work and HazeClan details- This story takes place on the banks of Lake Michigan. I did a lot of research on different bio types and landscapes to land on HazeClan, FloodClan, DuneClan, and BluffClan. HazeClan is the main POV of the story.

Lake Michigan is the only Great Lake located entirely within the United States, but we know that watersheds don't stop at the border! Lake Michigan is also home to the largest freshwater sand dunes in the world. Explore Lake Michigan data on DataStream: doi.org/10.25976/6fg...
The oak forests on the back side of the dune system along Lake Michigan is filled with those things.

To celebrate a half-century of preserving the Lake Michigan dunes, the Land Conservancy of West Michigan will launch a commemorative series that includes guided tours through one of the region’s most iconic landscapes. www.ottawanewsnetwork.org/guided-hikes...
The 120-foot Mount Baldy sits at the southern tip of Lake Michigan and moves about 5 to 10 feet every year.

Yeah, it's similar on the other side of Lake Michigan, top 2/3 of Wisconsin is mostly sand. Fun fact: there are sand dunes up to 40 feet high there that accumulate from winds after last ice age 11k years ago, hard to see from ground but clear from satellite LiDAR
It's very quaint to be sure, the Dunes area reminds me of my old stomping grounds along the western shore of Lake Michigan, lots of sand there and it has the same flavor, dune buggies and all that.
Thank you. I was wondering where the sand dunes were. I grew up in Michigan and spent my Summers on Lake Michigan where their Dunes are so high it remind you of

If you make it so Traverse City area - the cherry capital of the universe, I think 😜 - We have literally dozens of wineries with every kind of amazing setting, view, and architecture. Sleeping Bear Dunes id a must with sand dunes that rise 500 ft above Lake Michigan over turquoise and azure waters.

Yes, we have sand dunes, concrete sections of beach and sand beach even. It's quite the combo. And if you see any trash in these pictures, please know that I picked it all up. I usually pick up two bags of garbage along my walk. #beachfront #lakemichigan #chicago #sanddunes

A reminder of the sand dunes ecology of many areas around the Great Lakes, the path to get to the beach is cut through a sand dune. This lakefront is protected National Parks seashore (Indiana Dunes National Park).
I love big bear dunes or whatever it was and I love the great lakes. It is nice living a 3 minute drive from the beach even if it's cold lmao

.bsky.social - I'll post a series about Lake Michigan dune dynamics soon, but the short answer is: yes, those dunes were likely deposited from Glacial Lake Chicago by winds 6-10 thousand years ago. The image below is from Colgan et al. 2017, full citation & link in AltText.

The sand dunes located on the east shore of Lake Michigan are the largest freshwater dune system in the world. In multiple locations along the shoreline, the dunes rise several hundred feet above the lake surface.
